content: Management of antibiotic-resistant infections requires: 1) Source control (drainage, debridement, device removal); 2) Empiric therapy based on local antibiogram and patient risk factors; 3) Definitive therapy guided by culture/sensitivity results; 4) Combination therapy for certain resistant organisms (e.g., carbapenem-resistant Enterobacteriaceae). Antimicrobial stewardship programs reduce resistance through: prospective audit with feedback, formulary restrictions, diagnostic stewardship, dose optimization using pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic principles, and de-escalation when cultures return. CDC Core Elements include leadership commitment, accountability, drug expertise, action, tracking, reporting, and education.
